problem: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease?
mechanism: Optogenetic control of specific neuron populations
hypothesis: The key to Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease? is Optogenetic control of specific neuron populations — specifically Optogenetic control of — which would help 50,000,000 people affected.

experiment.protocol: Human organoid model with 36 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.
